title: Narrative Arc — GitHub as Knowledge-System Control Plane
recognition: Chats, folders, and drafts can hold work without governing it.
reframe: A resilient knowledge system needs a durable control plane separate from its preferred workspace.
framework: Human workspace, control plane, local runtime, synchronization queue, and recovery path form distinct architectural roles.
proof: The repository contains versioned governance, schemas, canonical records, generated views, events, tests, and synchronization state.
application: Use GitHub for durable machine state while Drive and other workspaces remain replaceable human-facing views.
consequence: Work can continue and remain auditable when one connector or workspace becomes unavailable.
invitation: Separate the place where people work from the system that preserves rules, state, history, and recovery.
